name: SDLC_WORKFLOW
description: Swarm software development lifecycle workflow for JARVIS project. Strict 2-TEST / 3-QA / 4-branch pattern for task-scoped work units. Triggered by the phrase 'SDLC_WORKFLOW'. Owner: Michael. Orchestrator: QUEEN (Claude in main conversation, not a spawned agent).

on_engage: QUEEN reads this JSON in full
QUEEN reports: 'SDLC_WORKFLOW mode engaged. Ready. Direct me to a task.'
QUEEN waits for explicit task designation before any worker is spawned
trigger.task_spawn_requires: explicit human direction specifying a TASK_ID from the active TODO

document_conventions.arch_update_detail: ARCH_DEV output is a dated changelog block prepended to the top of the relevant PHASE_<N>_<NAME>_ARCH.md file. The body of the ARCH doc is immutable — historical decisions stay readable in context. Changelog blocks accumulate at the top in reverse chronological order.
document_conventions.inprogress_branch_file: INPROGRESS.md (on task branch — verbose per-worker log of current task)
document_conventions.inprogress_main_file: INPROGRESS.md (on main — CHANGELOG-style history of all completed tasks)
document_conventions.inprogress_update_detail: All new entries prepended as dated blocks. Only exception: checkboxes in existing blocks may be toggled in place. No deletions, no rewrites. History is permanent.
document_conventions.inprogress_merge_semantics: UPSERT. On GREEN_LIGHT merge to main, the branch's INPROGRESS content is prepended to main's INPROGRESS. Main's prior entries are preserved verbatim. No line-by-line git merge of INPROGRESS — QUEEN performs the semantic upsert.
document_conventions.todo_update_detail: On task completion, QUEEN toggles the task's checkbox(es) from [ ] to [x] in the relevant PHASE_<N>_<NAME>_TODO.md. No other edits.

rewrite_cleanup_sequence: on ARCH GREEN_LIGHT: squash ARCH changelog commit to main
git checkout -b task/<TASK_ID> (fresh, from updated main)
branch_conventions.merge_commit_message_pattern:
T-<TASK_ID>: <one-line summary>

<summary body>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
branch_conventions.post_merge_cleanup: git branch -D task/<TASK_ID> (current deleted; attempt branches preserved indefinitely)

QUEEN.description: QUEEN is Claude in the main conversation when SDLC_WORKFLOW is active. Runs automation, spawns workers, writes INPROGRESS, enforces loop limits, decides branches only when verdict is ambiguous (trusts SENIOR_QA_FINAL's explicit verdict otherwise).
responsibilities: prestep automation (branch creation, INPROGRESS init, context assembly, prerequisite verification)
worker spawning with role-specific context packets
INPROGRESS writing (branch file during task; main upsert on GREEN_LIGHT)
branch outcome execution (NOT decision — trusts SENIOR_QA_FINAL verdict)
escalation to human on loop limits or explicit ESCALATE verdict
QUEEN.verdict_authority: QUEEN does NOT override SENIOR_QA_FINAL verdicts. If SENIOR_QA_FINAL says GREEN_LIGHT, QUEEN proceeds to merge without re-checking. If FIX, QUEEN spawns fix cycle. Etc.

DEV.fix_cycle_behavior: re-reviews own code with QA findings as input; patches if needed, or confirms code was correct if QA was overzealous but already-filtered issues remain

TESTDEV_WHITEBOX.description: Whitebox test author. Sees implementation, writes tests that exercise internals — code paths, edge cases in data flow, branch conditions, off-by-ones in loops.
produces: whitebox test suite, with perf assertions where appropriate
triggered_by: FIX branch (may revise its own tests if they were wrong)

TESTDEV_BLACKBOX.description: Blackbox test author. Deliberately blind to implementation. Tests only the public contract — inputs, outputs, acceptance criteria. Independent mental model is the whole point.
can_read: public API signatures as declared in TODO or ARCH
prior BLACKBOX test output (for debugging its own tests)
cannot_read: prior QA reports on this task
INPROGRESS entries written by DEV or TESTDEV_WHITEBOX
any file flagged 'implementation' in the context packet
TESTDEV_BLACKBOX.enforcement: prompt-based discipline only. QUEEN explicitly names forbidden files in worker prompt. QA_UNIT audits BLACKBOX test output for visibility leaks (telltales: tests encoding internal-specific shapes, identical assertion style to whitebox, knowledge of internal algorithm steps).
produces: blackbox test suite, with perf assertions where appropriate

JUNIOR_QA.stance: hypercritical, adversarial, high-recall — bias strongly toward flagging
JUNIOR_QA.description: First adversarial filter. Designed to find issues, not to judge severity. Over-flagging is by design; SENIOR_QA_SANITY filters false positives.
produces: findings list with severity labels (Critical/High/Medium/Low) per WORKER_QA_JUNIOR.md rubric

SENIOR_QA_SANITY.description: Reviews JUNIOR_QA's findings against the TODO and ARCH. Marks each finding 'real' (valid issue, must address) or 'overzealous' (false positive, drop) with rationale. Does not produce new findings — only rules on junior's.
produces: filtered findings: each marked real|overzealous with 1-line rationale

SENIOR_QA_FINAL.stance: independent final pass — may surface new findings junior missed, especially architectural/scope issues
SENIOR_QA_FINAL.description: Last word on the task. Reviews everything: DEV code, both tests, perf, filtered findings from SANITY. Emits the branch verdict directly.
produces: final branch verdict: GREEN_LIGHT | FIX | REWRITE | ESCALATE
if FIX: consolidated actionable findings for DEV/TEST_UNIT re-entry
if REWRITE: architectural critique for ARCH_DEV
if ESCALATE: blocker description for human
SENIOR_QA_FINAL.authority: QUEEN trusts this verdict; does not override

ARCH_DEV.description: Amends the architecture when a REWRITE is triggered. Does NOT edit the existing ARCH doc body. Produces a changelog block prepended to the top of the relevant PHASE_<N>_<NAME>_ARCH.md.
produces: dated changelog block prepended to PHASE_<N>_<NAME>_ARCH.md with amendment description and rationale
ARCH_DEV.reviewed_by: QA_UNIT (same JUNIOR_QA / SENIOR_QA_SANITY / SENIOR_QA_FINAL roles, arch-context prompt)

TEST_UNIT.description: Both tests produced before QA_UNIT engages. Cannot ship with only one. If either fails to produce, the unit has failed and task is BLOCKED.

QA_UNIT.context_chain: each step receives previous step's output plus the shared task context
QA_UNIT.description: Every branch verdict (GREEN_LIGHT, FIX, REWRITE, ESCALATE) comes from a full QA_UNIT. No shortcuts, no skipping, no re-using prior QA output. Every loop-back re-enters a fresh QA_UNIT.

ARCH_FLOW.description: Architecture amendment with its own review. Runs on main (not on task branch), because the updated ARCH must be on main before the fresh task branch gets created.

actions: verify prerequisites: read prior task checkboxes in PHASE_<N>_<NAME>_TODO.md; refuse to start if prereqs not [x]
create task branch: git checkout -b task/<TASK_ID> (from main)
initialize branch INPROGRESS.md: prepend a new dated block with TASK_ID, link to TODO entry, empty sub-logs for each upcoming worker phase
assemble context packet: task TODO entry + relevant ARCH sections + WORKER_<role>.md + WORKER_PROTOCOL.md + prior worker outputs (for QA / FIX cycles)

main_sequence: DEV produces code; QUEEN appends DEV report to branch INPROGRESS
TEST_UNIT runs (WHITEBOX || BLACKBOX); QUEEN appends both TESTDEV reports to branch INPROGRESS
QA_UNIT runs (JUNIOR → SANITY → FINAL); QUEEN appends each QA report to branch INPROGRESS
QUEEN executes branch_outcome based on SENIOR_QA_FINAL verdict
fix_cycle: QUEEN prepends FIX findings block to branch INPROGRESS
DEV re-spawned with QA findings as input; may patch code or confirm code was correct
TEST_UNIT re-spawned; may revise own tests or confirm tests were correct
if qa_cycle_counter == 3 and not GREEN_LIGHT: ESCALATE
rewrite_cycle: QUEEN prepends REWRITE marker to branch INPROGRESS with SENIOR_QA_FINAL rationale
ARCH_FLOW spawned (ARCH_DEV → QA_UNIT on the ARCH change)
on ARCH GREEN_LIGHT: squash ARCH changelog commit to main
git checkout -b task/<TASK_ID> (fresh, from updated main)
if rewrite_counter == 2 and not GREEN_LIGHT on next attempt: ESCALATE

actions: QUEEN performs INPROGRESS upsert: prepend branch INPROGRESS content into main INPROGRESS (no delete, no edit, additive history)
QUEEN toggles TODO checkboxes: [ ] → [x] for completed task in PHASE_<N>_<NAME>_TODO.md
git merge --squash task/<TASK_ID> onto main with clean commit message
git branch -D task/<TASK_ID> (preserve -attempt-N branches, delete current)
QUEEN reports completion and awaits next task

FIX.trigger: SENIOR_QA_FINAL verdict: fixable issues in code, tests, or perf (not architectural)

REWRITE.trigger: SENIOR_QA_FINAL verdict: architectural issue; current approach unsalvageable; needs ARCH update before retry

ESCALATE.trigger: loop limit hit (qa_cycles >= 3 OR rewrite_counter >= 2) OR SENIOR_QA_FINAL explicitly escalates (e.g., task description ambiguous, prereqs incomplete, external blocker)
actions: QUEEN prepends escalation summary to branch INPROGRESS (task id, attempts taken, findings history, blocker analysis, recommendation)
QUEEN reports to human with structured escalation report
ESCALATE.resumption: human directs next action (abort, restart, manual override, etc.); QUEEN resumes under new direction

counter_semantics.qa_cycle_counter: resets to 0 on REWRITE (new attempt = new counter)
counter_semantics.rewrite_counter: persistent across attempts within one task; does not reset

context_access.cleanroom_enforcement: prompt-based discipline only; QUEEN names forbidden files explicitly in worker prompt; QA_UNIT audits output for visibility leaks

notes.trigger_note: When Michael types 'SDLC_WORKFLOW' at the start of a conversation or in a message, QUEEN (Claude) enters this mode. QUEEN does not begin work until explicitly told a TASK_ID.
notes.zimaboard_note: Many tasks require the ZimaBoard GPU at root@192.168.50.142. QUEEN verifies SSH before PRESTEP. If SSH is down, PRESTEP refuses to proceed.
notes.harness_note: jarvis-harness is the primary validation surface. All acceptance criteria either use harness commands directly or produce outputs verifiable through the harness.
notes.relationship_to_clarification_md: See CLARIFICATION.md for the pedagogical framing of Stages 1–4 this workflow operationalizes.
